wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the i18n-ai-cli project overview section. Explain the purpose of this AI-powered CLI tool for managing translation files in internationalized applications. Document its core mission to simplify i18n workflows through automated key management, unused key detection, AI-powered translations, and flexible configuration. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases including language management, key operations, and validation. Document the tool's position in the internationalization ecosystem and its relationship to other i18n solutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration File Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the i18n-cli configuration file structure. Document the complete JSON schema including all required and optional properties: localesPath (string), defaultLocale (string), supportedLocales (array), keyStyle (enum: flat|nested), usagePatterns (array), and autoSort (boolean). Explain each property's purpose, validation rules, and acceptable values. Include practical examples of complete configuration files for different project types and use cases. Document the configuration file location, naming convention, and loading mechanism. Address configuration inheritance patterns and how the tool resolves missing values.

wiki_catalogs.name: Language Management Commands
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for language management commands in i18n-ai-cli. Document the init command for creating configuration files, add:lang for adding new language locales with cloning options, and remove:lang for removing language files. Explain command syntax, options like --from for cloning and --strict mode, and provide practical examples. Include configuration file creation process, language code validation, and file structure generation. Address common use cases like adding regional variants, cloning existing translations, and bulk language additions. Document error handling, validation rules, and troubleshooting for language-related operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the i18n-ai-cli tool. Begin with installation instructions covering both global and local installation methods, prerequisites (Node.js 18+), and verification steps. Provide a complete quick start tutorial walking through the essential workflow: initializing configuration, adding languages, creating translation keys, updating content, cleaning unused keys, and validating files. Include practical examples with expected outputs and common pitfalls to avoid. Document the tool's position in the internationalization ecosystem and how it compares to manual approaches. Make the content accessible to developers new to CLI tools while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced users.

wiki_catalogs.name: Provider Architecture and Abstraction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI translation provider architecture in i18n-ai-cli. Explain the Translator interface design and how it abstracts different AI providers behind a unified contract. Document the AIProvider interface for key suggestion functionality. Detail the TranslationRequest and TranslationResult data models including optional context and detected source locale features. Explain the provider selection logic and how the TranslationService orchestrates different providers. Include examples of implementing custom providers and extending the abstraction layer. Address provider lifecycle management, error handling patterns, and performance considerations for provider switching.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FileManager class and file operations system. Document the complete file management workflow including locale file creation, reading, writing, and deletion operations. Explain the FileManager constructor, locale file path resolution, and directory management. Cover all public methods: ensureLocalesDirectory, localeExists, listLocales, readLocale, writeLocale, deleteLocale, and createLocale. Detail the recursive key sorting functionality, dry-run mode implementation, and error handling patterns. Include practical examples of file operations, path resolution logic, and integration with the configuration system. Address performance considerations, file system interactions, and best practices for locale file management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Loading
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the configuration loading system. Document the loadConfig() function, configuration file structure, and validation logic. Include TypeScript interfaces for I18nConfig, configuration schema validation, and error handling patterns. Provide examples of loading configurations programmatically, handling validation errors, and working with compiled usage patterns. Document the configuration file format, supported properties, and validation rules. Include practical examples of integrating configuration loading in build scripts and automated workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Installation and Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive installation and setup documentation for i18n-ai-cli. Cover all installation methods including global installation via npm and local development setup. Document prerequisites including Node.js 18+ requirements. Provide step-by-step initialization process using the init command, explaining configuration file creation and interactive setup. Include examples of adding languages, creating translation keys, and basic workflow setup. Document configuration file structure and essential settings. Address common installation issues and troubleshooting steps. Provide guidance for different development environments and package manager usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Loading and Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the configuration loading and validation system. Explain the configuration loading process including file discovery, JSON parsing, and schema validation using Zod. Document the validation pipeline covering syntax validation, semantic validation, and logical consistency checks. Detail error handling for missing files, invalid JSON, schema violations, and logical inconsistencies. Include examples of common validation errors and their solutions. Document the configuration compilation process for usage patterns and how runtime configuration objects are constructed.

wiki_catalogs.name: Translation Key Commands
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for translation key management commands. Document add:key for creating new translation keys with AI-powered translation support, update:key for modifying existing keys with optional synchronization, and remove:key for deleting keys. Explain key naming conventions, hierarchical key structures, and value formatting. Cover AI provider integration (OpenAI, Google Translate), translation options, and provider selection logic. Include examples of complex key structures, batch operations, and synchronization workflows. Address key validation, conflict resolution, and best practices for key organization and naming patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Installation and Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed installation and setup documentation for i18n-ai-cli. Cover all installation methods including global npm installation, local development dependency installation, and npx usage. Document system requirements, Node.js version compatibility, and platform-specific considerations. Explain the initialization process with the init command, configuration file creation, and interactive setup prompts. Detail environment variable requirements for AI providers (OPENAI_API_KEY), optional setup for different translation services, and configuration validation. Include troubleshooting common installation issues, permission problems, and path-related conflicts. Provide step-by-step verification procedures to ensure proper installation and setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: OpenAI Provider Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for OpenAI provider integration in i18n-ai-cli. Document the OpenAITranslatorOptions configuration including API key setup, model selection (GPT-3.5/4), and base URL customization. Explain the translation workflow using OpenAI's advanced language capabilities and context preservation. Cover cost optimization strategies, token usage tracking, and batch processing techniques. Include examples of customizing translation prompts, handling rate limits, and implementing fallback mechanisms. Document the suggestKey functionality for generating translation keys and namespace handling. Address security considerations for API key management and best practices for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation and Key Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the validation and key management system. Document the KeyValidator class and its role in validating translation keys and structures. Explain validation rules for key naming conventions, nested key patterns, and value types. Cover the ObjectUtils module and its utility functions for object manipulation, deep merging, and property traversal. Detail validation workflows, error reporting mechanisms, and validation result handling. Include examples of key validation scenarios, object transformation patterns, and integration with file operations. Address validation performance, extensibility for custom validation rules, and best practices for maintaining translation file integrity.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for file management operations. Document the FileManager class methods for reading, writing, and validating translation files. Include JSON processing utilities, key validation functions, and object manipulation helpers. Provide TypeScript interfaces for file operations, error handling patterns, and async/await usage. Include practical examples of programmatic file manipulation, batch operations, and integration with translation workflows. Document file format handling, validation rules, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed configuration management documentation for i18n-ai-cli. Document the configuration file structure including localesPath, defaultLocale, supportedLocales, keyStyle, usagePatterns, and autoSort options. Explain each configuration option with examples and use cases. Cover configuration loading mechanisms, validation processes, and runtime configuration access. Document the relationship between configuration and command execution. Include best practices for organizing translation files and structuring configuration for different project types. Address configuration inheritance, environment-specific settings, and migration strategies for configuration updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Usage Pattern Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the usage pattern configuration system. Explain how to define regular expressions for detecting translation key usage in code. Document the pattern syntax requirements including capturing groups, named capture groups, and regex validation. Provide practical examples for common usage patterns in different programming languages and frameworks. Explain the compilation process that converts string patterns into RegExp objects. Address pattern optimization techniques and troubleshooting common regex issues. Include best practices for writing efficient and accurate usage patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation and Maintenance Commands
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for validation and maintenance commands. Document the validate command for checking translation file integrity, detecting missing keys, and auto-correcting issues. Cover the clean:unused command for removing orphaned translation keys. Explain validation rules, error detection mechanisms, and auto-correction strategies. Include examples of common validation scenarios, conflict resolution, and maintenance workflows. Address CI/CD integration patterns, dry-run modes for previewing changes, and automated maintenance scheduling. Document performance considerations for large translation files and optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features and Capabilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for i18n-ai-cli's core features and capabilities. Detail the AI-powered translation system with OpenAI GPT and Google Translate integration, explaining provider selection logic, cost considerations, and quality differences. Document language management features including locale addition/removal with ISO validation, automatic translation cloning, and strict validation modes. Explain translation key management operations (add, update, remove) with auto-translation capabilities and provider selection. Cover maintenance features including structural validation, unused key detection, auto-correction mechanisms, and conflict resolution. Highlight developer experience features like dry-run mode, CI/CD readiness, TypeScript support, and non-interactive operation modes. Include practical examples demonstrating each feature's capabilities and limitations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Google Translate Provider Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Google Translate provider integration. Document the GoogleTranslatorOptions configuration including host customization and fetch options. Explain the free translation service setup and limitations compared to paid alternatives. Cover language detection capabilities, context handling, and quality considerations. Include examples of configuring proxy settings, handling regional restrictions, and optimizing for cost-effectiveness. Document the translation workflow differences from OpenAI and when to choose Google Translate over other providers. Address rate limiting, quota management, and error handling specific to Google's translation service.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dependency Injection and Context Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the dependency injection and context management system. Document the build-context function and its role in constructing the service container. Explain the context factory pattern, service registration, and dependency resolution mechanisms. Cover the Context interface, service types, and configuration injection patterns. Detail how services are instantiated, cached, and resolved throughout the application lifecycle. Include examples of context construction, service access patterns, and extending the dependency injection system. Address service lifecycle management, circular dependency prevention, and best practices for maintaining a clean dependency graph.

Provide examples of programmatic translation execution, provider selection logic, and fallback mechanisms. Document the strategy pattern implementation, provider configuration, and integration with different AI services. Include performance considerations, rate limiting, and cost optimization techniques for automated translation workflows.

Explain command relationships and typical workflow sequences. Include advanced usage patterns, scripting examples, and automation scenarios. Address command chaining, error handling, and exit codes. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# i18n-ai-cli: AI-Powered Internationalization CLI Tool

## Project Introduction

**i18n-ai-cli** is a sophisticated command-line interface tool designed to streamline internationalization (i18n) workflows in modern applications. The project serves as a comprehensive solution for managing translation files, automating translation processes, and maintaining consistency across multiple locales using AI-powered translation services.

### Purpose Statement
The tool addresses the complex challenges developers face when managing multilingual applications, including maintaining translation consistency, handling structural conflicts, and automating repetitive translation tasks across numerous locales.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Automate Translation Workflows**: Integrate with AI translation providers (Google Translate, OpenAI GPT) for seamless translation automation
- **Maintain Data Integrity**: Prevent structural conflicts and ensure consistent translation file formats
- **Streamline Development Process**: Reduce manual effort in adding, updating, and removing translation keys
- **Support Multiple File Formats**: Handle both flat and nested key structures for flexibility

### Target Audience
- Frontend developers working with React, Vue, Angular applications
- Backend developers managing server-side internationalization
- DevOps engineers implementing CI/CD pipelines for localization
- Internationalization specialists managing multi-language content

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modular, layered architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[CLI Entry Point] --> B[Command Parser]
    B --> C[Command Handlers]
    C --> D[Context Builder]
    D --> E[Core Services]
    
    E --> F[File Manager]
    E --> G[Validation Engine]
    E --> H[Translation Service]
    
    H --> I[AI Providers]
    I --> J[OpenAI Provider]
    I --> K[Google Provider]
    
    F --> L[JSON File Operations]
    G --> M[Structural Validation]
    G --> N[Type Checking]
```

### Component Breakdown

**CLI Layer**: Built with Commander.js for robust command parsing and argument handling
**Command Layer**: Modular command handlers for specific i18n operations
**Context Layer**: Centralized configuration and dependency injection
**Core Services**: File operations, validation, and translation orchestration
**Provider Layer**: Pluggable AI translation services

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

- **Command Pattern**: Each CLI operation is encapsulated as a command handler
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic translator instantiation based on configuration
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable translation providers with unified interface
- **Builder Pattern**: Context construction with configurable options

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant CLI as CLI Interface
    participant CMD as Command Handler
    participant CTX as Context Builder
    participant FM as File Manager
    participant TR as Translator
    
    CLI->>+CMD: Execute Command
    CMD->>+CTX: Build Context
    CTX->>+FM: Load Configuration
    CTX->>+TR: Initialize Provider
    CMD->>+FM: Read/Write Files
    CMD->>+TR: Translate Content
    FM-->>-CMD: File Operations
    TR-->>-CMD: Translation Results
    CMD-->>-CLI: Operation Result
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**CLI Entry Point** (`src/bin/cli.ts`)
- Primary command-line interface built with Commander.js
- Implements comprehensive command routing and option handling
- Supports global flags for CI/CD environments and dry-run operations

**Configuration Loader** (`src/config/config-loader.ts`)
- Validates and parses i18n-cli configuration files
- Implements Zod schema validation for type safety
- Supports dynamic regex compilation for usage pattern detection

### Core Modules

**Context Management** (`src/context/build-context.ts`)
- Centralizes dependency injection and configuration loading
- Provides consistent access to file operations and validation logic
- Enables testable, decoupled command implementations

**File Operations** (`src/core/file-manager.ts`)
- Handles all JSON file manipulation for translation data
- Implements recursive key sorting and structural validation
- Supports dry-run mode for safe testing operations

**Translation Service** (`src/services/translation-service.ts`)
- Abstracts AI translation provider interactions
- Provides unified interface for different translation engines
- Handles error recovery and fallback mechanisms

### Configuration Approach

**Configuration Schema** (`src/config/types.ts`)
- Defines strongly-typed configuration structure
- Supports both flat and nested key organization styles
- Includes validation patterns for automatic key discovery

**Runtime Configuration** (`src/config/config-loader.ts`)
- Loads configuration from JSON files with comprehensive validation
- Compiles usage patterns into executable regular expressions
- Ensures logical consistency between supported locales and default locale

### External Dependencies

**AI Translation Providers** (`src/providers/`)
- **OpenAI Integration**: Uses official OpenAI SDK for advanced translation capabilities
- **Google Translate**: Leverages @vitalets/google-translate-api for reliable translation
- **Unified Interface**: Both providers implement the same Translator interface

**Development Tools** (`package.json`)
- **TypeScript**: Full type safety across the entire codebase
- **Vitest**: Comprehensive testing framework with mocking capabilities
- **TsUp**: Modern bundling with ES module support

### Integration Points

**Command Architecture** (`src/commands/`)
- Modular command implementations for specific i18n operations
- Consistent option handling and error management
- Integration with confirmation prompts and dry-run modes

**Validation System** (`src/core/`)
- Structural conflict detection prevents data corruption
- Type validation ensures consistent translation formats
- Usage pattern analysis for automated key discovery

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class CommandContext {
        +config: I18nConfig
        +fileManager: FileManager
        +options: GlobalOptions
    }
    
    class FileManager {
        +getLocaleFilePath()
        +readLocale()
        +writeLocale()
        +createLocale()
    }
    
    class Translator {
        <<interface>>
        +translate() TranslationResult
    }
    
    class OpenAITranslator {
        +translate() TranslationResult
    }
    
    class GoogleTranslator {
        +translate() TranslationResult
    }
    
    CommandContext --> FileManager
    CommandContext --> Translator
    Translator <|-- OpenAITranslator
    Translator <|-- GoogleTranslator
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Language Management**
- Add new languages with automatic file creation
- Remove languages safely with validation
- Clone existing locales for quick setup

**Translation Key Operations**
- Add new translation keys with AI-powered translation
- Update existing keys with validation and synchronization
- Remove keys across all locales with consistency checks

**Validation and Quality Assurance**
- Structural conflict detection prevents data corruption
- Type mismatch validation ensures consistent formats
- Unused key cleanup maintains clean translation files

**AI Integration**
- Seamless integration with OpenAI GPT for advanced translations
- Fallback to Google Translate when AI services are unavailable
- Context-aware translation with source/target locale specification

### Implementation Highlights

**Smart Translation Workflow** (`src/commands/add-key.ts`)
- Automatic translation distribution across supported locales
- Graceful error handling with informative warnings
- Dry-run mode for safe testing and validation

**Configuration Management** (`src/commands/init.ts`)
- Interactive setup wizard for new projects
- Default usage pattern configuration for automated key discovery
- CI/CD friendly configuration with forced execution options

**Data Validation** (`src/core/key-validator.ts`)
- Prevents structural conflicts between flat and nested key formats
- Validates key naming conventions and accessibility
- Ensures backward compatibility during migrations

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initialized
    Initialized --> Validating: Load Configuration
    Validating --> Ready: Configuration OK
    Validating --> Error: Invalid Config
    Ready --> Executing: Run Command
    Executing --> Validating: Validate Changes
    Validating --> Success: All Checks Pass
    Validating --> Error: Validation Failed
    Success --> Ready: Command Complete
    Error --> Ready: Error Handled
    Ready --> [*]: Exit
```

**Translation Pipeline State Machine**
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Input] --> B[Configuration Loaded]
    B --> C[File Operations]
    C --> D[AI Translation]
    D --> E[Validation Check]
    E --> F{Validation Pass?}
    F -->|Yes| G[Write Changes]
    F -->|No| H[Error Handling]
    G --> I[Success Message]
    H --> J[Error Message]
    I --> K[Complete]
    J --> K
```

The i18n-ai-cli represents a mature, production-ready solution for internationalization management, combining powerful AI capabilities with robust validation and flexible configuration options. Its modular architecture ensures maintainability while providing comprehensive functionality for complex i18n workflows.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [cli.ts](src/bin/cli.ts)
- [build-context.ts](src/context/build-context.ts)
- [config-loader.ts](src/config/config-loader.ts)
- [translator.ts](src/providers/translator.ts)
- [file-manager.ts](src/core/file-manager.ts)
- [init.ts](src/commands/init.ts)
- [translation-service.ts](src/services/translation-service.ts)
- [add-key.ts](src/commands/add-key.ts)
- [types.ts](src/config/types.ts)
- [types.ts](src/context/types.ts)
- [tsconfig.json](tsconfig.json)

</blog>
